title: Recapture of Tythe

{{Quote|It sounds to me as if the recapture of Tythe could prove very important toward ending the war. I don't understand all of it. The Jedi Council is being very secretive with me.|Supreme Chancellor Palpatine to Anakin Skywalker|<ref name="LoE" />}}
The '''recapture of Tythe''' took place on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tythe/Legends|Tythe]] in the [[19 BBY/Legends|third year]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], as part of the [[Outer Rim Sieges/Legends|Outer Rim Sieges]] campaign. Four [[Standard day/Legends|days]] after the [[Battle of Nelvaan]], [[High Jedi General/Legends|High Jedi General]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi General]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] were dispatched by [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], and Jedi Master [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] planet in an attempt to capture [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] leader [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]]. However, the whole engagement was, in truth, conducted by Palpatine (under the guise of [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]]) to draw the Jedi away from the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]] in advance of the [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|Separatist invasion of Coruscant]].

==Prelude==
{{Quote|Then move however many ships are needed to crush and occupy Tythe.|Darth Sidious, to General Grievous|<ref name="LoE">''[[Labyrinth of Evil]]''</ref>}}
In an attempt to throw [[Republic Intelligence/Legends|Republic Intelligence]] off his trail, [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] instructed [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] to send a small flotilla of [[Warship/Legends|warships]] to crush a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] on [[Tythe/Legends|Tythe]] and occupy the [[Planet/Legends|world]]. As the former headquarters of [[LiMerge Power/Legends|LiMerge Power]], a company connected to a secret [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] meeting area discovered in [[The Works/Legends|The Works]], Sidious knew that the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] would think that he was trying to erase the trail they had been following. As an added bonus, he had [[Count/Legends|Count]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] remain on Tythe as bait for [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]]. Ultimately, the entire engagement at Tythe was a diversion to draw Jedi away from the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]] in advance of a massive [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|Separatist invasion of Coruscant]].

Onboard a [[MedStar-class frigate/Legends|''MedStar''-class frigate]] orbiting [[Belderone/Legends|Belderone]], Kenobi and Skywalker (who had rescued the planet [[Nelvaan/Legends|Nelvaan]] four days earlier) received word from [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] Palpatine, who stated that Dooku should be captured, and arranged for Kenobi and Skywalker, along with a full Republic [[Battle group/Legends|battle group]], to retake Tythe.<ref name="LoE" />

==The battle==
{{Quote|The Council was able to convince Palpatine of the need to retake Tythe, and he has authorized a full battle group to divert there.|Obi-Wan Kenobi to Anakin Skywalker|<ref name="LoE" />}}
The [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatists]] had quickly placed mines at several [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] entry points, forcing the Republic [[Starship/Legends|ships]] to revert to [[Realspace/Legends|realspace]] in a narrow window in front of the [[Confederate Navy/Legends|Confederacy fleet]]. Both sides quickly deployed their [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] and then closed in on each other. The Republic battle group moved first, breaking to port and delivering a long range broadside to the Separatist vessels, which responded with their own barrage. The opposing starfighter squadrons regrouped and attempted to reach the enemy [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] before they could restore their [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]].<ref name="LoE" />

All this maneuvering by the Republic was a diversion, allowing [[Red Squadron (Galactic Republic)/Legends|Red Squadron]] to reach the surface of Tythe in order to capture Dooku alive. As a backup plan, it was deemed that saturation bombardment and limited starfighter engagement would send Dooku fleeing deeper into the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. Anakin Skywalker led Red Squadron through the Separatist fleet, managing to destroy a [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union]] [[Hardcell-class interstellar transport/Legends|starship]] on the way. Once they reached the lower atmosphere, defending [[IG-227 Hailfire-class droid tank/Legends|Hailfire droids]], [[Single Trooper Aerial Platform/Legends|STAPs]], and [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] assailed the incoming starfighters, managing to destroy all but the [[Eta-2 Actis-class interceptor/Legends|interceptors]] flown by the two Jedi.<ref name="LoE" />

Kenobi and Skywalker piloted their starfighters through the roof of the largest containment dome at the LiMerge Power facility, and then set off on foot to confront Dooku. Inside the enormous ruined archive hall, they encountered a life-sized [[Hologram/Legends|holoimage]] of the Count, but before they could locate him, hundreds of infantry droids surrounded them and opened fire. Unable to reach Dooku, Skywalker [[Force scream/Legends|called]] upon [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] with such ferocity that it brought down the ceiling of the vast hall. The Sith Lord managed to escape to his [[Solar sailer/Legends|solar sailer]] before the two Jedi could dig themselves out of the rubble, and ordered a Separatist retreat. Through the Force, however, Skywalker realized that Tythe was a trap to lure Jedi away from [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="LoE" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|Revealed, the importance of Tythe is. To draw into the Outer Rim additional Jedi.|Yoda to Mace Windu|<ref name="LoE" />}}
[[File:Coruscant_is_Under_Siege.jpg|thumb|right|300px|[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] learn of the [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|Battle of Coruscant]] from [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] aboard the [[Integrity (Venator-class)|''Integrity'']].]]
After the battle, the Separatist forces fled Tythe, with most ships jumping Rimward and others (led by Dooku) headed for the planet [[Nelvaan/Legends|Nelvaan]] (a planet that Kenobi and Skywalker had freed from Separatist forces four days prior) in the [[Koobi system]] in an attempt to ward off Republic pursuit. The Count then set course for Coruscant to join General Grievous, who had just managed to abduct Supreme Chancellor Palpatine.<ref name="LoE" /> Upon returning to the [[Integrity (Venator-class)|''Integrity'']], Skywalker and Kenobi were welcomed back by the ship's crew, who had begun to suspect that both were killed in the battle by the CIS forces.<ref name="LoE" />

Afterwards, Skywalker and his [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] made a number of modifications to the former's gold and black [[Mechno-arm/Legends|prosthetic arm]]. Kenobi then appeared, pointing out how his former Padawan wasn't in the medical lab, just as Skywalker was finishing tweaking his mechno-arm. Kenobi, sensing that his former Padawan was feeling emotional about their prior Nelvaan journey, relinquished to the young Jedi that the [[Jedi Trials/Legends#Trial of the Spirit|Trial of Spirit]] (where one would "look inside [themselves]") was the most difficult trial a Jedi must face, and while what a Jedi saw inside themselves was almost never pleasant, it was their decisions that shaped their destinies.<ref name="CW25">{{CW|25}}</ref>

Suddenly, R2 began to sound off. A message from [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] was projected, stating that the Chancellor had been kidnapped by [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] during the [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|Battle of Coruscant]]. Skywalker, realizing the situation had worsened, ordered the crew to refuel their star fighters and prepare to jump to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]].<ref name="CW25" /> The two Jedi then set course for Coruscant and jumped to the Core ahead of their battle group.<ref name="LoE" /> After the two reached Coruscant, they joined the battle against the Separatists, leading an assault on Grievous' flagship the [[Invisible Hand/Legends|''Invisible Hand'']].<ref name="ROTS" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Third Battle of Tythe first appeared in the novel ''[[Labyrinth of Evil]]'' by [[James Luceno]], published [[January 5]], [[2005]]. Both the animated series ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'' and the novel contradict each other.<ref name="SW.com Forums 1">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=196444&start=255#10054094|thread=EU Leadup to Revenge of the Sith|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2005-03-26|time=7:57 AM}}</ref> In the novel, Skywalker and Kenobi search for Darth Sidious and pursue Dooku on Tythe, yet in the series, they investigate a possible base for Grievous on frigid Nelvaan (which Dooku only pauses at in the novel). Initially, [[Leland Chee]] [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] this by stating that the Nelvaan events occurred after Tythe,<ref name="SW.com Forums 2">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=196444&start=285#10095419|thread=EU Leadup to Revenge of the Sith|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2005-03-29|time=10:49 AM}}</ref> but this was overwritten by ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', which fixed the events the other way around.<ref name="NEC" /> This article goes by the ''New Essential Chronology'' fix, integrating details from both ''Labyrinth of Evil'' and ''Clone Wars'' wherever possible.

==Appearances==
*''[[Labyrinth of Evil]]'' {{1st}}
